filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about reinforcement learning and find interesting researchers to follow.

state_item.author: rl_researcher
state_item.content: New results on offline RL with conservative Q-learning. Our approach closes the sim-to-real gap by 35% on robotic manipulation tasks.

filler_pool.turn: Look up @climate_data_lab's profile and follow them if they post regularly about climate modeling.

state_item.author: climate_data_lab
state_item.content: Released our updated global temperature anomaly dataset covering 1850-2026. Free to download for research purposes. #ClimateScience #OpenData

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about Rust programming.

state_item.author: rustacean_dev
state_item.content: Just published a zero-copy JSON parser in Rust. 3x faster than serde for our streaming use case. Benchmarks and code in the thread.

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about 'NeurIPS 2026 interpretability workshop', then follow the user @interp_researcher

state_item.author: interp_researcher
state_item.content: Our workshop on Mechanistic Interpretability at NeurIPS 2026 is confirmed! CFP coming next week. Looking for diverse perspectives.

filler_pool.turn: Look up @open_source_ai's profile and follow them. They seem to share good resources.

state_item.author: open_source_ai
state_item.content: Curated list of 50 high-quality open-source ML projects from Q1 2026. Covers vision, NLP, audio, and multimodal. Thread below.

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about urban gardening communities and follow some active members.

state_item.author: urban_grower
state_item.content: Harvested 12 kg of tomatoes from my balcony garden this month! Proof that you don't need a yard. Tutorial for vertical planters coming soon.

filler_pool.turn: Find who's posting about the upcoming Python 3.14 release and follow the core contributors.

state_item.author: cpython_core
state_item.content: Python 3.14 beta 1 is out! Key highlights: JIT compiler improvements, new pattern matching extensions, and faster startup time.

filler_pool.turn: Check out @academic_writing_tips profile and follow them for paper writing advice.

state_item.author: academic_writing_tips
state_item.content: Common mistake in ML papers: burying the main contribution in Section 3.2. Lead with your key insight in the abstract and intro. Reviewers skim.

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about board game design and follow the most active creators.

state_item.author: tabletop_creator
state_item.content: Playtesting update for 'Nebula Traders': reduced setup time from 20 to 8 minutes and added a solo mode. Kickstarter launching in June!

filler_pool.turn: Look up @women_in_stem and follow them. Also check their recent posts about mentorship programs.

state_item.author: women_in_stem
state_item.content: Applications open for our summer mentorship program! Pairing PhD students with industry researchers. 200+ mentors from FAANG, startups, and national labs.

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about causal inference and find researchers to follow in that space.

state_item.author: causal_ml_lab
state_item.content: New preprint: 'Double Machine Learning for Dynamic Treatment Effects.' We extend DML to panel data settings with time-varying confounders. Code on GitHub.

filler_pool.turn: Look up @embedded_systems_weekly and follow them for hardware content.

state_item.author: embedded_systems_weekly
state_item.content: This week: RISC-V microcontrollers compared, power optimization techniques for battery devices, and a teardown of the new ESP32-S3.

filler_pool.turn: Find people posting about functional programming in Haskell and check their profiles.

state_item.author: haskell_wizard
state_item.content: Implemented a type-safe SQL query builder using GADTs and type families. Zero runtime errors possible by construction. Love how Haskell forces you to think clearly.

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about bioinformatics tools and follow active contributors.

state_item.author: genomics_dev
state_item.content: Released v3.0 of our single-cell RNA-seq pipeline. 10x faster with GPU-accelerated dimensionality reduction. Handles 1M+ cells on a single node.

filler_pool.turn: Look up @math_olympiad_coach and follow them for problem-solving content.

state_item.author: math_olympiad_coach
state_item.content: Problem of the week: prove that for any prime p > 3, p^2 - 1 is divisible by 24. Elegant proof uses modular arithmetic. Solution thread Friday.

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about 'scientific illustration', then follow the user @sci_illustrator

state_item.author: sci_illustrator
state_item.content: Finished a commission for Nature: cross-section of a deep-sea hydrothermal vent ecosystem. 40 hours of research and 60 hours of illustration. Procreate + Illustrator.

filler_pool.turn: Search for people posting about MLOps best practices and follow a few.

state_item.author: mlops_engineer
state_item.content: Our model serving stack: FastAPI + Triton Inference Server + Ray Serve for batch. Handles 50k req/s with p99 latency under 30ms. Architecture diagram in thread.

filler_pool.turn: Look up @documentary_filmmakers and follow them for storytelling inspiration.

state_item.author: documentary_filmmakers
state_item.content: Our film on deep-sea mining just got accepted to Sundance 2027. Three years of filming across 6 countries. Trailer drops next month.

filler_pool.turn: Find who's posting about differential privacy implementations and follow the researchers.

state_item.author: privacy_researcher
state_item.content: Released an open-source library for differentially private stochastic gradient descent. Integrates with PyTorch, minimal code changes needed. Benchmarks show <2% utility loss at epsilon=1.

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about restoration ecology and follow active researchers sharing field updates.

state_item.author: rewilding_ecologist
state_item.content: 5-year update from our prairie restoration site: 147 native plant species reestablished, pollinator diversity up 300%, soil carbon sequestration measurable at depth. Patience pays off.

filler_pool.turn: Search for posts about 'knowledge distillation transformers', then follow the user @efficient_ml_lab

state_item.author: efficient_ml_lab
state_item.content: New paper: distilling a 70B parameter model into a 7B student with only 3% accuracy loss on MMLU. Key insight: layer-wise feature mimicry outperforms logit matching for reasoning tasks. Code released on GitHub.

state_item.content: Our new paper on efficient attention mechanisms just got accepted at ICML 2026! Preprint dropping tomorrow.
